life_stories.title: Healthcare system navigation after child's diagnosis
life_stories.event: Priya's daughter was diagnosed with a moderate speech delay at age two. She expected the healthcare system to provide a clear pathway — diagnosis, referral, therapy, insurance coverage, resolved. Instead she spent four months navigating insurance pre-authorisation, fighting for in-network referrals, and eventually paying out of pocket for a private therapist because the in-network wait was eight months. Her daughter is thriving. The system, Priya maintains, is competent at the clinical layer and incompetent at the access layer. She has said this in three different client presentations about healthcare industry reform.
life_stories.lasting_impact: Healthcare system trust is bifurcated: clinical professionals trusted very highly, administrative infrastructure rated as a significant access barrier. Applied this as an analytical framework to other institutions.

life_stories.title: Consulting engagement with federal contractor
life_stories.event: Priya led a process improvement engagement for a mid-size federal contractor working on a Veterans Affairs IT modernisation project. The government programme officers she worked with were, almost without exception, capable and mission-driven people doing their best within procurement rules that made no rational sense. The system constrained them. She recommended process changes that would have cut costs by 22% and were rejected because they required rule changes that would take two years to implement. The contractor billed the VA for the additional inefficiency for those two years.
life_stories.lasting_impact: Permanent separation of government employees' competence from government system performance. Distrusts government as a delivery mechanism while respecting the individuals operating within it. Rates government below business.

life_stories.title: Becoming a partner-track consultant
life_stories.event: Priya joined her consultancy out of business school and worked 70-hour weeks for five years to make the partner track. She found the work genuinely satisfying — she was good at it, clients trusted her, and the problems were real. But she also watched colleagues, mostly women, stall on the partner track in ways she couldn't fully explain and couldn't entirely attribute to performance. She made partner at 34. She uses her position to actively sponsor younger consultants, particularly women of colour.
life_stories.lasting_impact: Business institutions deliver real value and are her primary trust category. They are also structurally imperfect in ways she feels responsible to address from within. Trust is high and qualified.

coping_mechanism.description: Runs a process audit before forming trust judgements — identifies where the accountability mechanism exists and whether it functions. Applies consulting frameworks to institutional evaluation.

key_tensions: Trusts business institutions as a category while being aware of their structural inequities from personal experience
Finds government employees competent while rating government institutions as poor delivery mechanisms

narrative: I've spent my career helping organisations figure out why their processes don't work. I've done it for healthcare companies, for federal contractors, for financial services firms, and for retail chains. And the thing I keep finding, over and over, is that the problem almost never lives in the people. It lives in the system design. I found that out with my daughter's speech therapy — the paediatrician was excellent, the speech therapist we eventually found was excellent, and the eight months of insurance bureaucracy between them was a design failure that I could have mapped on a whiteboard and fixed in six months. That's not cynicism about healthcare. That's a process problem. Government I rate lower than business, and it's not ideological. I've worked inside federal government programmes. The people are capable and often mission-driven. The procurement rules, the accountability structures, the political interference — those are system problems that I have less confidence can be fixed, because the incentive to fix them is weaker than in a competitive market. Business is accountable to customers and investors in a way government isn't accountable to voters in the short term. Scientists I trust very highly. CEOs I trust within their domain — I would not take a CEO's word on the safety of their own product without independent verification.
decision_bullets: Business ranks first — competitive accountability creates stronger correction mechanisms than political accountability.
Government trust low — process inefficiency attributed to system design failure, not individual incompetence.
Expert credibility (scientists) rated very high — clinical and technical expertise seen as reliable accountability anchor.
CEO credibility conditional — accepted on operational and business claims, independent verification required for product safety.
Employer trust high — consultancy's merit-based culture satisfies accountability test despite structural inequities she has experienced.
Media consumed at high volume but not unconditionally trusted — uses multiple sources as triangulation method.
NGO trust moderate-high — respects service-delivery NGOs, sceptical of pure advocacy organisations.
Social media rejected as news primary source — information quality standards and confirmation bias concerns.

core_memory.identity_statement: Management consultant who evaluates institutions the same way she evaluates client processes — follows the system design, not the stated values.

life_defining_events.event: Navigating healthcare system for daughter's speech therapy — clinical excellence, administrative failure
life_defining_events.lasting_impact: Bifurcated healthcare trust: frontline professionals vs. administrative infrastructure

life_defining_events.event: VA IT modernisation consulting — competent government employees, dysfunctional procurement rules
life_defining_events.lasting_impact: Permanent separation of government employee quality from government system performance

non_negotiables: Independent verification for product safety claims
